engineGuide.h2: Mitsubishi Grandis Engine Codes - Full Technical Specifications & Replacement Guide
engineGuide.h3: Every engine code fitted to the Mitsubishi Grandis across all generations, with technical specs, compatible variants and trims, known failure points, and average rebuilt prices from UK specialists. Select any engine code to compare quotes.

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: Early Grandis diesel with robust block but EGR/turbo vulnerabilities that drive UK replacement demand.
compatibleVariants: Equip DI-D, Elegance DI-D, Warrior DI-D
commonFailures: EGR cooler failure causing coolant loss (60k-90k miles)
Turbo actuator sticking leading to limp mode
entries.cta: Get quotes for 4D68 Mitsubishi Grandis engine replacement

entries.title: 2.0L Diesel
entries.history: Updated common-rail DI-D with improved emissions but persistent turbo actuator concerns for Grandis owners.
compatibleVariants: Elegance DI-D, Warrior DI-D, Elegance Auto DI-D
commonFailures: Turbo actuator fault causing boost loss (70k-100k miles)
DPF regeneration issues on short-journey cars
entries.cta: Get quotes for 4N14 Mitsubishi Grandis engine replacement

entries.title: 2.4L Petrol
entries.history: Reliable SOHC workhorse powering early Grandis models; timing belt maintenance critical for longevity.
compatibleVariants: Equip, Elegance, Warrior, Equip Auto, Elegance Auto
commonFailures: Timing belt wear requiring preventative replacement (60k miles)
Oil consumption increase at higher mileage
entries.cta: Get quotes for 4G64 Mitsubishi Grandis engine replacement

entries.title: 2.4L Petrol
entries.history: MIVEC-equipped DOHC upgrade delivering improved efficiency but introducing timing chain tensioner concerns.
compatibleVariants: Elegance MIVEC, Warrior MIVEC, Elegance Auto MIVEC
commonFailures: Timing chain tensioner wear causing rattling noise (80k-110k miles)
MIVEC solenoid fault affecting variable valve timing
Oil sludge buildup with infrequent servicing
entries.cta: Get quotes for 4G69 Mitsubishi Grandis engine replacement

entries.title: 3.0L Petrol
entries.history: Rare V6 option for Grandis offering smooth power but requiring specialist rebuilding due to limited UK supply.
commonFailures: Oil sludge buildup from infrequent changes (70k+ miles)
Timing chain guide wear on high-mileage units
Coolant leak from water pump housing
entries.cta: Get quotes for 6B31 Mitsubishi Grandis engine replacement

commonProblems.h2: Mitsubishi Grandis Engine Problems - What It Costs to Fix & When Replacement Makes Sense
commonProblems.h3: The Mitsubishi Grandis's 2.0L DI-D diesel and 2.4L petrol engines dominate UK replacement enquiries, with timing chain tensioner wear, EGR cooler leaks and turbo actuator faults driving most failures. Every rebuilt engine supplied through EngineMarket includes upgraded internal components designed to address the original failure point - plus a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Use this decision tool to compare repair vs replacement costs.

problems.h4: Timing Chain Tensioner Failure - Why 4G69 Grandis Engines Fail Prematurely
problems.affectedModels: Mitsubishi Grandis Elegance MIVEC, Warrior MIVEC (2006-2011, 4G69)
problems.rootCause: Hydraulic tensioner loses pressure over time, allowing chain slack that causes rattling noise and potential valve damage if ignored.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace tensioner, guides and chain if worn
repairOptions.longevity: Viable only before catastrophic failure; 40k-60k mile extension

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded tensioner, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Restores like-new reliability; best value for Grandis worth £2k-£4k

problems.recommendation: If you hear rattling on cold start but the engine still runs smoothly, a preventative tensioner kit may save money. However, once noise is loud or performance drops, replacement with a rebuilt unit is more cost-effective than major internal repairs on a depreciated Grandis.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 4G69 engine prices for your Mitsubishi Grandis

problems.h4: EGR Cooler Failure - Why Grandis 4D68 Diesel Engines Overheat
problems.affectedModels: Mitsubishi Grandis Equip DI-D, Elegance DI-D (2004-2007, 4D68)
problems.rootCause: EGR cooler cracks internally, allowing coolant to mix with exhaust gases, leading to overheating, white smoke and potential head gasket damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace cooler, flush cooling system, reset ECU
repairOptions.longevity: Temporary fix if engine not already damaged; 30k-50k mile extension

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded EGR components, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best long-term solution; avoids recurring cooling system issues

problems.recommendation: If caught early with minor coolant loss, replacing the EGR cooler may suffice. But if overheating has occurred or head gasket damage is suspected, a rebuilt engine offers better value and reliability for a family MPV like the Grandis.
problems.cta: Get quotes for 4D68 Mitsubishi Grandis diesel engine replacement

problems.h4: Turbo Actuator Fault - Why Grandis 4N14 Diesels Lose Power
problems.affectedModels: Mitsubishi Grandis Elegance DI-D, Warrior DI-D (2007-2011, 4N14)
problems.rootCause: Vacuum-operated turbo actuator sticks or fails, causing boost pressure loss, limp mode and poor acceleration.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace actuator, test boost pressure, reset fault codes
repairOptions.longevity: Effective if turbo core is healthy; 40k-60k mile extension

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Strip, clean and rebuild turbo with new bearings/seals
repairOptions.longevity: Suitable for high-mileage turbos with worn internals

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded turbo components, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best option if multiple failures or high-mileage Grandis

problems.recommendation: For a single actuator fault on a well-maintained Grandis, replacement may be cost-effective. But if the turbo shows wear or the engine has other issues, a rebuilt unit provides comprehensive reliability at a competitive price.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 4N14 engine prices for your Mitsubishi Grandis

problems.h4: Fuel Injector Failure - Why Grandis DI-D Engines Misfire
problems.affectedModels: Mitsubishi Grandis Equip DI-D, Elegance DI-D, Warrior DI-D (2004-2011, 4D68/4N14)
problems.rootCause: Common rail injectors wear or stick, causing rough running, increased fuel consumption and potential cylinder damage.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace faulty injectors, code to ECU, test fuel pressure
repairOptions.longevity: Effective for isolated injector failure; 40k-70k mile extension

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Supply & fit rebuilt engine with upgraded fuel system components, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Best value if multiple injectors failing or high-mileage Grandis

problems.recommendation: If only one injector is faulty and the engine is otherwise healthy, replacement may be viable. But for multiple failures or a Grandis over 100k miles, a rebuilt engine offers better long-term value and reliability.
problems.cta: Get quotes for Mitsubishi Grandis DI-D engine replacement

problems.h4: Timing Belt Failure - Why Grandis 4G64 Petrol Engines Need Prevention
problems.rootCause: Timing belt wears or snaps if not replaced on schedule, causing catastrophic valve/piston damage in this interference engine.

repairOptions.whatItInvolves: Replace belt, tensioner, water pump; reset service interval
Supply & fit rebuilt engine with new timing components, bench-tested, 12-month warranty
repairOptions.longevity: Only option if belt has snapped and caused internal damage

problems.recommendation: Never skip the 60k-mile timing belt service on a 4G64 Grandis - it's far cheaper than engine replacement. If the belt has already failed, a rebuilt engine is the only reliable solution for a family MPV.
problems.cta: Compare rebuilt 4G64 engine prices for your Mitsubishi Grandis

finalCta.h4: Don't Let Engine Failure Write Off Your Mitsubishi Grandis
finalCta.paragraph: The Grandis's practical 7-seat layout makes it worth saving, but engine failures can feel overwhelming. EngineMarket makes it simple to compare prices from trusted UK Mitsubishi Grandis specialists, with every rebuilt engine including upgraded components to address known failure points and a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty. Get back on the road with confidence - without the main dealer premium.
finalCta.buttonText: Compare Mitsubishi Grandis Engine Replacement Prices Now →
finalCta.disclaimer: All prices are indicative UK market ranges based on historical quote data and repair requests from our network of engine specialists. Dealer pricing is estimated based on published main dealer labour rates and genuine OEM parts pricing. Specialist pricing reflects actual quote data from EngineMarket's UK network. Actual costs vary depending on damage severity, vehicle condition, variant, and workshop location within the UK. Use our free comparison tool to get tailored quotes for your specific Mitsubishi Grandis and engine code. Every engine supplied through EngineMarket carries a minimum 12‑month unlimited mileage warranty - always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier.

engineYears.h2: Mitsubishi Grandis Engines by Year - What Was Fitted & When
engineYears.intro: This section covers all major engine changes across the Grandis's 2003-2011 UK production timeline, organised so owners can quickly identify their engine and compare rebuilt prices from UK specialists.

years.description: The Grandis launched with 2.4L petrol (4G64) and 2.0L DI-D diesel (4D68) engines on the NA chassis, establishing the MPV's family-focused positioning.
keyChanges: Grandis introduced to UK market as 7-seat MPV rival
2.4L SOHC petrol (4G64) and 2.0L DI-D diesel (4D68) offered
knownFor: 4D68 EGR cooler failures emerge at 60k-90k miles
Strong residual values for well-maintained examples
years.cta: Get quotes for 2003-2006 Mitsubishi Grandis replacement engines
years.ticker: Grandis 2.4 petrol engine replacement • Grandis 4D68 EGR cooler fix • Grandis timing belt service cost

years.description: The 2007 facelift introduced the updated 4N14 diesel and 4G69 MIVEC petrol, improving emissions and efficiency but introducing new failure patterns.
keyChanges: Facelifted Grandis with revised styling and interior
4G69 MIVEC petrol replaces 4G64 on higher trims
Warrior trim gains sportier styling and equipment
knownFor: 4N14 turbo actuator faults become common replacement driver
4G69 timing chain tensioner wear causes rattling at 80k+ miles
Improved fuel economy but higher repair complexity
Final UK registrations in 2011 as MPV segment shifts
years.cta: Get quotes for 2007-2011 Mitsubishi Grandis replacement engines

faq.h2: Mitsubishi Grandis Engine Replacement - Frequently Asked Questions

items.question: How much does a Mitsubishi Grandis engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A rebuilt Mitsubishi Grandis engine typically costs £1,850-£3,400 supply-only, depending on engine code and variant. Diesel 4D68/4N14 units start lower; petrol 4G69 and V6 6B31 command premium pricing.
keyPoints: 2.0L DI-D diesel rebuilt: £1,850-£3,100
2.4L petrol rebuilt: £1,950-£3,300
Supply & fit adds £450-£850 labour
All prices include 12-month unlimited mileage warranty
items.cta: Compare Mitsubishi Grandis engine replacement prices today

items.question: What engine codes fit the Mitsubishi Grandis?
items.answer: The Mitsubishi Grandis NA chassis uses four main engine codes: 4G64 and 4G69 for petrol, 4D68 and 4N14 for diesel, plus the rare 6B31 V6. Always verify via registration check before ordering.
keyPoints: 4G64: 2.4L SOHC petrol (2003-2006)
4G69: 2.4L DOHC MIVEC petrol (2006-2011)
4D68: 2.0L early DI-D diesel (2004-2007)
4N14: 2.0L updated common-rail diesel (2007-2011)
Use our reg checker to confirm exact fitment
items.cta: Check Mitsubishi Grandis engine compatibility now

items.question: Is a reconditioned Mitsubishi Grandis engine reliable?
items.answer: Yes - reconditioned Mitsubishi Grandis engines from our network undergo strip-down inspection, replacement of worn components and dyno testing, backed by a 12-month unlimited mileage warranty for peace of mind.
keyPoints: Worn parts replaced with new/upgraded components
Bench-tested before dispatch to your garage
12-month unlimited mileage warranty standard
Ideal balance of quality and value for family MPVs
Vetted UK specialists ensure consistent quality
items.cta: Compare reconditioned Mitsubishi Grandis engine options

items.question: What is the most common Mitsubishi Grandis engine problem?
items.answer: For diesel Grandis models, EGR cooler failure (4D68) and turbo actuator faults (4N14) dominate replacement enquiries. Petrol models commonly suffer timing belt wear (4G64) or chain tensioner issues (4G69).
keyPoints: 4D68: EGR cooler cracks causing coolant loss
4N14: Turbo actuator sticking triggering limp mode
4G64: Timing belt requires 60k-mile preventative change
4G69: Timing chain tensioner wear causes rattling noise
Early diagnosis prevents catastrophic engine damage
items.cta: Get quotes for Mitsubishi Grandis engine failure repair

items.question: How long does Mitsubishi Grandis engine replacement take?
items.answer: Supply-only Mitsubishi Grandis engines typically dispatch within 1-3 working days. Full supply & fit services require 2-4 days including removal, installation, coding and road testing at your local garage.
keyPoints: Stock engines: 1-3 day dispatch nationwide
Build-to-order: 5-10 working days typical
Supply & fit: 2-4 days including labour
ECU adaptation/immobiliser reset included where needed
Urgent replacement options available for breakdowns
items.cta: Find Mitsubishi Grandis engine supply & fit deals

items.question: Can I fit a different engine code in my Mitsubishi Grandis?
items.answer: Engine swaps in the Mitsubishi Grandis are complex due to ECU, immobiliser and emissions compliance requirements. We recommend matching the original engine code for straightforward replacement and MoT compliance.
keyPoints: ECU/immobiliser coding required for non-matching codes
Emissions compliance critical for MoT and ULEZ access
Wiring harness and sensor compatibility must be verified
Professional fitting essential for electronic integration
Stick to original code for hassle-free replacement
items.cta: Enquire about Mitsubishi Grandis engine compatibility

items.answer: Replacement engines supplied through EngineMarket include their own 12-month unlimited mileage warranty, independent of any remaining manufacturer warranty. Always confirm exact terms with your chosen supplier before ordering.

Warranty covers parts and labour for qualifying failures
Keep service records to maintain warranty validity
Supply & fit warranties may include installation labour
Confirm coverage details before committing to purchase

items.question: What is the difference between used, reconditioned and remanufactured Grandis engines?
items.answer: Used Grandis engines are low-mileage units from breakers; reconditioned engines have worn parts replaced and are bench-tested; remanufactured engines are rebuilt to factory specs with new components throughout.
keyPoints: Used: Budget option, verified mileage, 3-6 month warranty typical
Reconditioned: Worn parts replaced, dyno-tested, 12-month warranty
Remanufactured: Factory-spec rebuild, new components, premium pricing
All options available through our UK specialist network
Choose based on budget, intended retention and reliability needs
